title: Coleostephus myconis
taxonomy: Coleostephus myconis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It is native to the Mediterranean region and is often found growing in dry, rocky areas. Within the family Asteraceae, Coleostephus myconis is classified under the subfamily Carduoideae, which includes thistle and lettuce-like plants. Further classification places it in the tribe Cardueae, a group of plants characterized by their prickly stems and leaves. The specific classification of Coleostephus myconis is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Asterids, order Asterales, family Asteraceae, subfamily Carduoideae, tribe Cardueae, genus Coleostephus, and species C. myconis.

Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. intro: Coleostephus myconis, also known as Myconis or Myconis Flower, is a small, daisy-like flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Coleostephus myconis include flavonoids, such as luteolin and apigenin, as well as sesquiterpenes and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Coleostephus myconis is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and it is also used in topical applications, such as creams and salves, to support skin health and reduce inflammation.
